page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-16 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The instance is equipped with 124.0 GB of system memory, providing a high resource density of 7.75 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or bundled GPU accelerators, and comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The underlying x86_64 architecture supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512, Intel AMX for matrix operations, and hardware-accelerated cryptography via AES and SHA_NI.

In benchmark evaluations, the c4-highmem-16 demonstrates strong single-threaded performance, achieving top-tier results in Geekbench single-core testing and single-threaded gzip compression. Text processing also ranks in the top tier. Multi-core and memory-bound workloads generally perform in the average range, with a Passmark CPU score of 24,574.11 and a multi-core Geekbench score of 13,151.0. Large-block memory read bandwidth reaches 111.3 GB/sec, while write bandwidth is measured at 50.44 GB/sec. For AI workloads, the instance achieves top-tier text generation speeds of 317.01 tokens/sec on small 135M LLM models, though performance scales down to average levels for larger models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c4-highmem-16 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio without the added cost overhead of local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware. This makes it a balanced choice for workloads requiring substantial memory capacity relative to compute resources. The instance is well-suited for memory-intensive applications such as database operations, caching layers running Redis, web serving, and medium-scale software compilation. It is also capable of handling lightweight LLM inference and data processing tasks that benefit from its advanced vector and matrix instruction sets.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-16 is an x86_64 virtual machine instance configured with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 124.0 GB of RAM, yielding 7.75 GB of memory per core. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor running at 2.3 GHz, it features 8 physical cores and a 260 MB L3 cache. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. In benchmarks, it delivers top-tier single-threaded performance, notably in Geekbench single-core and gzip compression, while multi-core operations and memory bandwidth perform in the average tier. Lacking local disks, it relies on network storage. The c4-highmem-16 is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, including database operations, Redis caching, and lightweight language model inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-16 is an x86_64 instance featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 124 GB of RAM. It delivers top-tier single-core performance for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-16 features 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 124 GB of RAM, optimized for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 virtual machine offering high memory density and top-tier single-core performance for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor at 2.3 GHz.
124.0 GB of system memory providing 7.75 GB of RAM per vCPU.
Top-tier single-core performance in Geekbench and gzip compression benchmarks.
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix and vector operations.
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators.
Optimized for database operations, Redis caching, and software compilation.
